name: Naná Vasconcelos
abstract: Juvenal de Holanda Vasconcelos, known as Naná Vasconcelos (2 August 1944 – 9 March 2016), was a Brazilian percussionist, vocalist and berimbau player, notable for his work as a solo artist on over two dozen albums, and as a backing musician with Pat Metheny, Don Cherry, Jan Garbarek, Egberto Gismonti, Gato Barbieri, and Milton Nascimento.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Brought Gary into the band and Nana. I liked the way Nana played percussion and sang so he was a special guest. ... Then we did “Preludio Pra Nana” which was a piece I wrote for Nana.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
